summary: Crystal‑clear audience remaster captures a stellar second set with scorching Scarlet Begonias, Fire on the Mountain and an epic Playing in the Band.
review: Both reviewers rave about this 6/18/83 partial‑set2 release. The Beerengineer remaster turns a previously muddy audience tape into an audibly crisp recording that lets every note of Jerry’s guitar and Phil’s bass bombs shine, while the second set delivers highlight performances of Scarlet Begonias, Fire on the Mountain, Playing in the Band, The Wheel and the Dew. Overall the show is praised as deep‑improvisational, focused and emotionally powerful.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews consistently describe an excellent remastered audio source and a top‑tier second‑set performance; weighting the strong show quality (70%) with the superb recording (30%) yields a high overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remaster removes previous muffiness, delivers crisp clarity on Jerry’s solo and Phil’s bass bombs; no noticeable drop‑outs or distortion reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set described as deep‑improvisational with sustained momentum, tight group focus and seamless transitions that keep the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work is highlighted as crystal‑clear after the remaster; earlier recordings had his solo obscured.
band_member_comments.Phil: Massive bass “bombs” are praised for their power and presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming is noted as elite, contributing to the hour‑plus improvisational flow.
